recent.statement: Flash flooding will occur in the Pittsburgh area due to heavy rainfall
recent.evidence: No captured signal concerns this forecast, and flash flooding of this scale in Pittsburgh would have been captured if it occurred.

recent.statement: A M6+ earthquake will strike the state of Oaxaca in Mexico
recent.evidence: The only Mexico earthquake signal was 5.7M (below M6) and not confirmed to be in Oaxaca, so no M6+ Oaxaca event was captured.

recent.statement: A M6.0 earthquake will strike the southern East Pacific Rise within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: No signal reports any M6.0 earthquake on the southern East Pacific Rise; all listed quakes are elsewhere and below magnitude 6.0.

recent.statement: A M5+ earthquake will strike the Caribbean Sea within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: No signal reports any earthquake in the Caribbean Sea; all listed quakes occurred elsewhere (Scotia Sea, Peru, Mexico, Japan).

recent.statement: A M5+ earthquake will strike near the southern East Pacific Rise.
recent.evidence: No signal reports an earthquake near the southern East Pacific Rise; the quakes listed occurred in Scotia Sea, Peru, and Mexico.

recent.statement: A magnitude 6.0 earthquake will strike near Puerto Madero, Mexico
recent.evidence: The only Mexico earthquake signal shows a magnitude 5.7 event, below the forecast 6.0 threshold and not specified near Puerto Madero, so no signal confirms the event occurred.

recent.statement: A significant oil price spike will occur within the next 24 hours due to disruptions at the Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint.
recent.evidence: Brent Crude is flat at 88.1 $/bbl (+0.0%) with no signal indicating any Bab el-Mandeb disruption or oil price spike.

recent.statement: A magnitude 6.5 earthquake will strike near the coast of Mexico within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: The only Mexico earthquake signal (S3) was magnitude 5.7M, below the required 6.5 threshold, and no signal shows a 6.5M event.

recent.statement: A M5+ earthquake will strike near the Puerto Madero area in Mexico
recent.evidence: S3 reports a M5.7 earthquake generically in 'Mexico' but does not name Puerto Madero, so it fails to confirm the event at the specified location.

recent.statement: The US will experience a major cyberattack within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: No captured signal reports a major US cyberattack, an event of that scale would certainly have been captured, so it did not occur within the window.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will strike the southern East Pacific Rise within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: No signal reports an earthquake on the southern East Pacific Rise; the captured quakes were in the Scotia Sea, Peru, and Mexico.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will strike near the city of Los Angeles in the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: The only signals describe a magnitude 5.5 earthquake in the Scotia Sea, not near Los Angeles, so no M5.0 LA event was captured within the window.
